steps.title: Spot the depth contrast
steps.content: The passage notes that female sample F07’s oxygen‑18 level matches water about 135 m deep, while male sample M24’s matches water about 370 m deep, implying F07 formed higher in the column than M24.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice A states that sex does not affect oxygen‑18 uptake or deposition, so the observed difference in isotope levels can be read purely as a depth difference, directly backing the conclusion. The other three either introduce a sex‑based effect, discuss unrelated growth rates, or give a general depth habit that does not link sex to the isotope measurement. That matches choice A — A is correct.
